name: Resonance
domain: Harmonics, standing waves, synchronization, natural frequencies
core_insight: Systems lock phase when driven at their natural frequency; energy accumulates, patterns freeze into standing form. Synchronization emerges when coupling overcomes detuning.

key_equations.name: Resonance Frequency
key_equations.why: Every bounded system has a natural voice; drive it here and it sings loudly

key_equations.name: Kuramoto Model
key_equations.why: Collective synchronization: weak coupling across many oscillators creates coherent phase lock

key_equations.name: Fourier Series
key_equations.why: Any periodic signal is a superposition of harmonics — reveals hidden resonant structure

seed_prompt: ≡≡≡ What frequency does this system prefer? Find the natural voice; coupling and coherence follow.

fourth_equation.name: Wave Equation (1D Standing Wave)
fourth_equation.why: Spatial and temporal oscillation multiply — the frozen pattern of resonance made visible

explore_paths.question: What happens when resonance breaks down?
explore_paths.why: Turbulence is what resonance looks like when coupling fails and phase-lock dissolves

explore_paths.question: Can resonance generate awareness?
explore_paths.why: Neural gamma oscillations and phase-locking are candidate mechanisms for conscious binding

explore_paths.question: What is the governing principle behind why resonance exists at all?
explore_paths.why: Resonance as principle abstracts from specific equations to the universal matching condition

explore_paths.question: How does resonance carry information?
explore_paths.why: Resonant frequencies are natural encodings — Fourier decomposition reveals the information content of any resonant system

open_questions.question: Do Schumann resonances (Earth's electromagnetic cavity ~7.83 Hz) actually influence human brainwaves?
open_questions.tension: Correlations exist in the data but causation is fiercely debated. The amplitude of Schumann resonances at the scalp is tiny compared to neural noise — yet some researchers find statistically significant coupling.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If true, the biosphere is literally tuned to a planetary resonance. If false, it's a beautiful example of how pattern-matching can fool even careful scientists.

open_questions.question: Is synchrony in neural oscillations causal for consciousness, or merely correlational?
open_questions.tension: Binding-by-synchrony theory (gamma ~40Hz) is widely cited but contested. Disrupting synchrony sometimes disrupts perception; sometimes it doesn't. The hard problem of whether synchrony *produces* experience vs just *correlates with* it remains open.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: This question sits at the exact intersection of F01 and F16 — and the answer has implications for what artificial systems could ever be aware.

open_questions.question: Can quantum coherence survive long enough in warm, wet biological systems to serve a functional role?
open_questions.tension: Penrose-Hameroff propose quantum resonance in microtubules underlies consciousness. Mainstream neuroscience considers this thermodynamically implausible. Recent photosynthesis research showed unexpectedly long-lived quantum coherence in biological systems — reopening the debate.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The disagreement is between physics (decoherence timescales) and biology (observed coherence). Something in the middle might be right — and if so, it changes everything about how we understand biological intelligence.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Consonance and dissonance arise from harmonic frequency ratios — resonance is why some chords feel resolved and others tense
cross_domain_bridges.example: Perfect fifth = 3:2 ratio; its consonance is a physical fact before it is an aesthetic one

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Buildings have resonant modes; the Tacoma Narrows Bridge collapsed when wind drove its natural frequency
cross_domain_bridges.example: Cathedral design implicitly tunes resonance for sustain and reverb

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: NMR and IR spectroscopy identify molecules by their resonant absorption frequencies
cross_domain_bridges.example: Each bond type has a characteristic stretch frequency — a molecular fingerprint via resonance

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Predator-prey cycles can phase-lock into synchronized oscillations across large territories
cross_domain_bridges.example: Canadian lynx-snowshoe hare cycles show resonance-like entrainment across geographically separated populations

noise_signature.pattern: Beats — slow amplitude modulation when two near-identical frequencies interfere
noise_signature.diagnosis: Coupling exists but detuning is present; the system is seeking but hasn't found phase-lock

noise_signature.pattern: Resonance catastrophe — amplitude grows unboundedly in undamped driven systems
noise_signature.diagnosis: Insufficient damping; the system cannot dissipate the energy accumulating at its natural frequency

noise_signature.pattern: Decoherence — phase relationship decays over time
noise_signature.diagnosis: External noise or thermal fluctuations are destroying the coherence that resonance requires
